findings.evidence: `TauCeti/LowDimTopology/Plumbing/Characteristic.lean:163` defines `isCharacteristicVector_iff_forall_modEq_intersectionForm` with conclusion `(∑ v, k v * x v) ≡ P.intersectionForm x x [ZMOD 2]`; `Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.lean:72` defines `dotProduct ... := ∑ i, v i * w i`, so the new theorem at `Weight.lean:55` is the same statement after unfolding `dotProduct`.
findings.fix: Delete this theorem and use `(P.isCharacteristicVector_iff_forall_modEq_intersectionForm k).mp hk x` with `simpa [dotProduct]` at the call site.
findings.issue: `IsCharacteristicVector.dotProduct_modEq_intersectionForm` is a duplicate wrapper around the existing all-vector characteristic parity theorem, differing only by writing the covector evaluation as `k ⬝ᵥ x`.

summary: The PR no longer duplicates finite-function dot product, but it still adds a parallel characteristic-parity theorem already provided by the plumbing characteristic API up to the existing `dotProduct` notation.
